attrs.title: UI Concept Model
attrs.purpose: Classify controls, elements, aggregations, associations, and events.

attrs.text: Controls and elements have stable semantic names.

attrs.title: Application Structure
attrs.purpose: Describe the required structure of applications built on the framework.

attrs.text: Applications compose pages, dialogs, forms, lists, and navigation containers from library-published components rather than from framework-private primitives.
The framework must support a navigable shell-level structure and a page hierarchy built entirely from public components discoverable through the library catalog.
Library dependencies must be explicit so that the application structure can be resolved by reading the dependency declaration and the public component catalog alone.

attrs.title: Layout System
attrs.purpose: Describe the abstract layout and composition requirements.

attrs.text: Layout is expressed as ordered or named composition regions exposed through aggregations.
Responsive behavior must preserve component composition semantics when layout density, theme, or viewport changes.
Container components may expose drag-and-drop semantics only when declared in metadata.
Components expose stable identity, owning library, public metadata, and typed properties, aggregations, associations, and events.

attrs.definition: a public, named unit published by a library
a typed configuration value with a default and visibility
a non-owning reference to another component
a named notification with typed parameters

attrs.title: Interaction Model
attrs.purpose: Describe how user interaction is represented in the specification.

attrs.text: User-triggered behavior is modeled as named events with stable semantics, such as press for activation.
Interaction events describe component-level intent rather than raw browser gestures.
Application handlers attach to the public event contract and must not depend on private DOM events, renderer internals, or browser-specific event ordering.

attrs.title: State Model
attrs.purpose: Describe state held or exposed by components.

attrs.text: Component state is represented by typed properties with explicit default values.
State may be public or hidden, but only public state participates in the external component contract.
Defaulted state and derived state must remain compatible with the declared metadata type system.

attrs.title: Data Binding Model
attrs.purpose: Describe how component state binds to external models.

attrs.text: Bindable properties and aggregations are part of the component contract and must be explicitly declared with a bindable flag.
Property bindings connect a single typed value, while aggregation bindings connect a model collection to an owned aggregation through a template.
Every binding resolves against a default or named model path, and async updates preserve declared types.

attrs.title: Form Model
attrs.purpose: Describe fields, labels, validation, and submission semantics.

attrs.text: Fields expose validation state.

attrs.title: Navigation Model
attrs.purpose: Describe route and navigation item semantics.

attrs.text: Navigation items reference valid pages.
Feedback messages have severity and lifecycle.

attrs.title: Accessibility Model
attrs.purpose: Describe labels, descriptions, focus, and keyboard support.

attrs.text: Interactive elements expose accessible names.

attrs.title: Theming / Design Tokens
attrs.purpose: Describe reusable color, spacing, and typography tokens.

attrs.text: Theme tokens map to runtime CSS custom properties.
Translatable public text must be referenced through stable message keys resolved from locale-specific message resource bundles rather than hard-coded literals.
Message resource bundles must resolve through a deterministic locale fallback chain from the most specific locale to a default bundle.
Locale-sensitive values such as dates, numbers, and currencies must render through typed, locale-aware formatting rather than fixed presentation.
The active locale and text direction must be externally configurable application-level settings that components inherit rather than hard-code.

attrs.title: Security / Privacy UI Rules
attrs.purpose: State UI-facing security and privacy constraints.

attrs.text: User-provided text, URLs, and style values are untrusted input and must be encoded, validated, or constrained before rendering.
Components that reveal sensitive values must default to masked output and require explicit reveal actions.
Irreversible or restricted actions require confirmation and permission gates before invoking application behavior.

attrs.title: Performance Requirements
attrs.purpose: Describe performance-oriented expectations that follow from the public model.

attrs.text: Public library catalogs must allow eager discovery of available components without eagerly loading every implementation detail.
Immutable public projections, such as the API projection and catalog metadata, must be cacheable by a stable identity.
Components that own large or unbounded collections through aggregations must support virtualization with observable materialization budgets.

attrs.title: Extension Model
attrs.purpose: Describe supported extension points, extension artifacts, and compatibility gates.

attrs.text: Extension points declare accepted artifact type, multiplicity, ownership, and host capability gates before runtime content is rendered.
Extension artifacts publish runtime metadata and optional design-time overlays without changing the host component public API.
Renderer and drag-and-drop capabilities are explicit metadata and must not depend on private DOM contracts.

attrs.title: Compliance Rules
attrs.purpose: Summarize what every implementation must satisfy.

attrs.text: Every public library member must be discoverable from a stable machine-readable catalog entry.
Every public component must declare complete public metadata for properties, aggregations, associations, events, defaults, visibility, and capabilities.
Compliance evidence must stay synchronized across documentation, generated examples, tests, and generated projections.

attrs.name: catalog-discoverability
attrs.meaning: Public components resolve from catalog traversal evidence.

attrs.name: metadata-completeness
attrs.meaning: Public metadata contains every required member group.

attrs.name: contract-based-validation
attrs.meaning: Validation checks the public contract rather than private renderer details.

attrs.name: cross-cutting-evidence
attrs.meaning: Accessibility, theming, internationalization, security, performance, extension, and tests remain synchronized.

attrs.name: testable-conformance
attrs.meaning: Compliance rules emit diagnostics that automated tests can assert.

attrs.name: explicit-capability-scope
attrs.meaning: Capabilities are declared before generated output depends on them.

attrs.definition: an implementation whose public catalog, metadata, generated projection, and evidence satisfy all mandatory OpenUI rules
the cataloged components, metadata, events, aggregation contracts, and generated projections that applications may rely on
documentation, generated examples, tests, and projection artifacts that demonstrate conformance
the declared set of capabilities and rules an implementation claims to satisfy
a missing or stale catalog, metadata, documentation, generated example, projection, or test artifact
a generated or tested file that records a compliance decision in a repeatable form

attrs.text: Catalog verification diagnostics identify the missing catalog entry and traversal node.
Metadata verification diagnostics identify the component and missing public metadata group.
Evidence verification diagnostics identify stale generated examples and test artifacts.
Documentation verification diagnostics keep prose synchronized with generated examples.
Generated projections verification diagnostics compare api-json projection artifacts with the public contract.

attrs.title: Catalog discoverability check
attrs.description: Validates that sample.library.Button is reachable from library-catalog-root and library-component-catalog traversal nodes.

attrs.title: Metadata completeness gate
attrs.description: Checks properties, aggregations, associations, events, defaults, visibility, and capability metadata before generation succeeds.

attrs.title: Cross-cutting evidence record
attrs.description: Synchronizes accessibility, theming, internationalization, security/privacy, performance, extension, and acceptance-test evidence.

attrs.title: Generator compliance guard
attrs.description: Fails generation with diagnostics when catalog traversal, metadata completeness, or synchronized evidence is missing.

attrs.title: Test & Acceptance Criteria
attrs.purpose: Describe how the specification should be verified.

attrs.text: Acceptance criteria remain traceable to specification sections and evidence artifacts.

attrs.title: Reference Examples
attrs.purpose: Provide concrete reference components abstracted from upstream evidence.

attrs.text: Reference examples connect component contracts to observable behavior.
